hero.name: Tu N (Alex) Tran
hero.headline: Graduate student in Engineering Data Science and AI building intelligent tools for biological and multimodal data analysis.

featured.title: Loki Interactive Inference Platform
featured.intro: Developed a full-stack web platform on top of Loki, an AI framework for spatial transcriptomics and histopathology analysis. The platform enables researchers to run deep learning inference on gigapixel whole-slide H&E images directly from the browser — no local Python environment or CLI required.
bullets: Built a Dash/React-based interface for launching deep learning inference workflows on gigapixel whole-slide H&E images from the browser.
Connected frontend interactions with backend pipelines for nuclei segmentation and cell-type annotation, removing the need for local setup or CLI-based execution.
Implemented interactive visualization of whole-slide images with scalable rendering and color-coded biological overlays.
Added an LLM-assisted interpretation feature to help users query segmented cell populations and tissue microenvironments in natural language.

paragraphs: I am a Graduate student in Engineering Data Science and AI with experience applying statistical modeling, machine learning, and data visualization to biological and engineering problems.

I am highly skilled in Python, R, SQL (in progress), and cloud-based workflows. My core expertise lies in building end-to-end pipelines, cleaning and structuring large datasets, and developing precise interactive dashboards.
Currently, I am seeking a Data Science internship to leverage my analytical skills and programming expertise for data-driven decision making.

education.degree: Master of Science in Engineering Data Science and AI
education.school: University of Houston, Cullen College of Engineering

projects.title: Loki Interactive Inference Platform
projects.description: Developed a full-stack web platform for spatial transcriptomics and histopathology. Built a Dash/React interface for deep learning inference on gigapixel H&E images directly from the browser.

projects.title: Mjolnir
projects.description: Built an interactive data visualization platform using Python (Dash, Plotly) for exploration of multimodal datasets. Integrated gigapixel histology images with gene expression and pathway analysis to enable intuitive exploration by non-technical users.

projects.title: Visual–Omics Curation
projects.description: Curated a multimodal dataset linking histology images with spatial gene expression across 32 organs. Built diagnostics to evaluate machine learning models, heavily contributing to a Nature Methods publication.

projects.title: Macrophage Analysis
projects.description: Applied Leiden clustering and UMAP to identify and interpret immune cell subtypes from single-cell datasets. Developed Python pipelines for reproducible exploratory analysis.

experience.company: Houston Methodist, Dr. Guangyu Wang Lab
bullets: Built data pipelines for large-scale single-cell and spatial omics datasets to support machine learning model development and evaluation
Applied unsupervised learning and statistical analysis to identify patterns in high-dimensional biological data
Assessed model predictions and validated outputs against biological signals to ensure reliability and interpretability
Developed interactive dashboards for visualization and interpretation of model results by research teams
Contributed to a Nature Methods publication on AI-driven multimodal analysis
Monitored digital engagement metrics with Google Analytics and optimized campaigns for +39.5% performance improvement.
Designed and launched targeted LinkedIn, Facebook, and Google Ads campaigns, raising user engagement by 50%.
Produced clear, data-driven reports to inform leadership and refine product strategy.

publications.title: A visual–omics foundation model to bridge histopathology with spatial transcriptomics
publications.authors: Chen W., Zhang P., Tran T. N., et al. (2025). Nature Methods.

publications.title: Thor: a platform for cell-level investigation of spatial transcriptomics and histology
publications.authors: Zhang P., Chen W., Tran T. N., et al. (2025). Nature Communications, 16, 7178.

items: Python (Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, scikit-learn, Plotly, Dash)

contact.paragraph: I am seeking a Data Science internship to leverage my analytical skills and programming expertise for data-driven decision making. Let's connect!
